question: In native chemical ligation (NCL), what type of cysteine-containing peptide fragment is required at the ligation junction?
options: An N-terminal cysteine on the C-terminal fragment
A C-terminal thioester on the N-terminal fragment reacting with an N-terminal cysteine
A C-terminal cysteine on the N-terminal fragment
An N-terminal thioester on the C-terminal fragment
explanation: NCL requires a C-terminal thioester on one peptide fragment reacting with an N-terminal cysteine on the adjacent fragment. The thiol group of cysteine undergoes transthioesterification followed by an S-to-N acyl shift, forming a native peptide bond.
